title: Off Paper, Onto Water

mood.label: Cautiously hopeful
mood.summary: The war ends on paper and the strait reopens, markets and oil exhale and a malaria vaccine and a library on Juneteenth shine — but fresh fire in Lebanon and Ukraine and Africa's hard ledger of Sudan and Ebola hold the day at the hopeful edge of the middle.

style.genre: neo-soul / dub-tinged — sampled marimba leading a warm topline over a spacious TR-808 with tape-echo, soft muted-brass stabs, a plucked upright bass and a sine sub, with a bowed-string pad underneath; C# mixolydian's lowered seventh keeping the brightness honest and not quite settled

sampleSources: Strudel default library (sampled VCSL marimba and bowed strings, GM muted brass, sampled upright/electric bass, RolandTR808 drum machine, sine sub-bass)
pattern:
stack(
  s("bd ~ ~ ~ bd ~ ~ ~").bank("RolandTR808").gain(0.62).hpf(40).room(0.14),
  s("~ ~ ~ ~ cp ~ ~ ~").bank("RolandTR808").gain(0.3).room(0.34).delay(0.2).delaytime(0.375),
  s("hh ~ hh hh ~ hh ~ hh").bank("RolandTR808").gain(perlin.range(0.05,0.13).slow(9)).pan(0.62).swingBy(0.08, 4),
  s("~ ~ rim ~ ~ ~ ~ rim").bank("RolandTR808").gain(0.22).pan(0.38).room(0.3).swingBy(0.08, 4),
  note("cs1 ~ ~ ~ gs1 ~ ~ b1").s("sine").attack(0.01).release(0.4).lpf(360).gain(0.46),
  note("cs2 ~ <gs2 fs2> ~ as1 ~ ~ b1").s("gm_acoustic_bass").gain(0.42).lpf(900).room(0.12),
  note("<[cs3,es3,gs3,b3] [fs3,as3,cs4,e4] [gs2,b2,ds3,fs3] [as2,cs3,es3,gs3]>").s("gm_muted_trumpet").attack(0.04).release(0.7).sustain(0.6).gain(0.2).lpf(sine.range(800,1900).slow(15)).room(0.34).rsize(5).delay(0.14).delaytime(0.375).pan(0.46).slow(2),
  note("<[cs2,gs2] [fs2,cs3] [gs1,ds2] [as1,es2]>").s("gm_string_ensemble_1").attack(1.3).release(2.8).sustain(0.85).gain(0.16).lpf(sine.range(620,1300).slow(20)).room(0.5).rsize(7).slow(2),
  note("cs5 ~ <ds5 es5> ~ gs5 ~ ~ b4 ~ <as4 fs5> ~ es5 ~ ~ cs5 ~").s("marimba").gain(0.26).lpf(3400).room(0.4).rsize(5).pan(0.4).delay(0.28).delaytime(0.5).off(0.125, x => x.gain(0.11).pan(0.6)).slow(2)
)

lines: The war came off the page in Geneva and the strait let the tankers through,
no tolls on the narrow water, a blockade lifted, a sky gone almost blue;
oil slid to a three-month low and the Nasdaq caught its breath and climbed,
and for one Friday morning the world read its own good news on time.
So I'll sing it off paper, onto water, the lowered seventh keeping me honest and slow —
cautiously hopeful, the bright edge of the middle, a peace I don't yet wholly know;
raise the marimba over the long string drone, let the small good things ring through:
a vaccine for the children, a library on Juneteenth, and the strait breathing open and new.
But south of the line they traded fire and Kyiv woke to drones again,
and Africa carried the heaviest ledger — a thousand gone, and Ebola's hundred and ten;
so I won't pretend the chord resolves, I'll let the seventh hang in the air,
and hold the hope at the edge of the middle, the way an honest day would dare.
